articles.title: Trump & Bibi watch closely as Mojtaba Khamenei meets Pezeshkian in rare secret talks
articles.description: Amid growing rumours of internal rifts within Iran’s leadership, President Masoud Pezeshkian has confirmed a rare, nearly 2.5-hour meeting with Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei. The encounter marks the first public contact since the February 28 strikes and comes as reports suggest tensions between the civilian government and IRGC commanders over war policy. Pezeshkian is said to have privately criticised escalating military actions as risky for Iran’s economy. With ceasefire talks intensifying and the U.S. pushing for a deal, this high-level meeting could signal a critical shift in Tehran’s strategy. Is Iran recalibrating or deepening its internal divide?

articles.title: Is Trump's Gulf ally paying the price for backing America as Iran rains fire on its oil lifeline?
articles.description: The Gulf may have just crossed into its most dangerous phase yet. Missiles in the sky. Oil zones in flames. And Arab capitals suddenly confronting a question they never thought they would have to ask: is the UAE now directly in Iran’s crosshairs? The region has witnessed a dramatic escalation. The UAE says its air defenses were activated against incoming Iranian missiles and drones. Fujairah, one of the Gulf’s most critical oil hubs, was suddenly engulfed in flames after a mysterious strike. And at the center of the crisis sits a pipeline that may matter more than the Strait of Hormuz itself. Because this was not just any target. Reports suggest the attacks focused on the UAE’s key oil bypass route, infrastructure specifically designed to reduce dependence on Hormuz and weaken Tehran’s leverage over Gulf shipping. Iran denies responsibility. Abu Dhabi blames Tehran. Tehran blames Trump’s “adventurism.” And somewhere in the middle of the accusations lies a larger geopolitical battle over oil, pressure, and control of the Gulf. But this crisis did not emerge in isolation. The UAE had already exited OPEC+, increased oil ambitions, and moved closer to Washington. Now, analysts are asking whether those moves unintentionally painted a target on Abu Dhabi at the worst possible moment. As drones, tankers, pipelines, and warships become part of the same battlefield, one thing is becoming clear: this is no longer just a Hormuz crisis. It is a fight over who controls the future flow of global energy.

articles.title: Skyroot hits unicorn status, but India's private space race needs clearer skies
articles.description: Hyderabad's Skyroot Aerospace has become India's first space launch vehicle unicorn. This achievement fuels the nation's space technology sector. The company is preparing for its Vikram-1 rocket launch. This development signifies a major shift for Indian businesses. The space sector presents a significant economic opportunity. However, government decisions regarding technology transfer could impact private companies like Skyroot and Agnikul Cosmos.

His work highlights the deep connection possible with animals, moving beyond mere observation. Attenborough championed evolution, revealing the beauty in life's journey. He transformed nature documentaries, always prioritizing the natural world over himself.

articles.title: Why TVK chief Vijay has to wait to become Tamil Nadu Chief Minister | Tell Me Why | Episode 41
articles.description: Tamil Nadu politics has taken a dramatic turn after actor-turned-politician Vijay’s TVK emerged as the single largest party, defeating both DMK and AIADMK. But why is there still no Chief Minister? The controversy now centers on Governor Rajendra Arlekar, who has refused to invite Vijay to form the government without proof of majority support. Constitutional experts argue the Governor must first invite the single largest party, making this a major debate over democracy, constitutional norms, and political strategy. Is this constitutional caution or political interference? Here’s a full breakdown of the unfolding Tamil Nadu power struggle.

articles.title: African proverb of the day: 'No matter how beautiful and well crafted a coffin might look, it will not...' – why no one can escape death and lessons on why no luxury, wealth and outward beauty can hide pain, suffering and sorrow

The proverb reminds us to look beyond superficial appearances and recognize the inherent preciousness of life.

articles.title: “We Will Outlast Them”: Prof. Marandi Warns U.S. Of A Long War; Slams UAE For Aiding Ground Invasion
articles.description: From the heart of Tehran, India Today speaks with Professor Mohammad Marandi on the escalating 39-day-old conflict. Marandi asserts that despite heavy damage to Iranian petrochemical and pharmaceutical infrastructure, the leadership remains unified. He characterizes the U.S. "maximum pressure" and naval blockade as a global economic catastrophe that Iran is prepared to outwait. Marandi specifically warns neighboring regimes, like the UAE, that hosting U.S. ground troops and drones makes them direct participants in the war. Defiant and resolute, he emphasises that for Iran, this is a "fight for survival" that they are destined to win.
